title: Permutations, Combinations and Probability
subject: Mathematics
content:
# Permutations, Combinations and Probability

## Quick Synopsis

Counting is about whether order matters and whether repetition is allowed. Probability adds event logic and conditional thinking.

## Must Know

- Permutation means arrangement; combination means selection.
- Complement counting often simplifies restrictions.
- Identical objects require division by factorials of repeated counts.
- Conditional probability updates sample space.
- Independent events satisfy P(A ∩ B) = P(A)P(B).
- Bayes theorem reverses conditional probability using partitions.

## Formulas / Rules

- nPr = n!/(n-r)!; nCr = n!/[r!(n-r)!].
- Circular permutations of n distinct objects = (n-1)! around a circle.
- P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A ∩ B).
- P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B)/P(B).
- Bayes: P(Ai|B) = P(Ai)P(B|Ai) / ΣP(Aj)P(B|Aj).
- Binomial probability: P(X=r) = nCr p^r q^(n-r).

## JEE Focus

- Break problems into cases only when cases are disjoint.
- Use gap method for non-adjacent arrangements.
- Use inclusion-exclusion for at least/onto/distribution problems.
- Convert probability to favorable count over total count when outcomes are equally likely.

## Common Traps

- Do not multiply cases that are alternatives; add them.
- Arrangement around necklace may need reflection adjustment, unlike circular table.
- Independence and mutual exclusiveness are different.
- Conditional probability denominator is the condition event.

## 10-Minute Revision Checklist

- Can you decide permutation vs combination?
- Can you handle identical objects?
- Can you use conditional probability?
- Can you apply binomial distribution?
